name: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS
summary: expressive text-to-speech API available now in Gemini API and AI Studio — 200+ audio tags for directing delivery ([slowly, with gravity]), 70-language support, SynthID watermarking; free tier available

name: HuggingFace + GitHub Copilot Chat
summary: open-source models now plug into GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code — swap in Qwen, Llama, or any HF model as your in-editor AI assistant without leaving your IDE

name: Claude Managed Agents + ant CLI
summary: public beta for building and deploying Claude agents — sandboxed execution, checkpointing, scoped permissions, SSE streaming; new ant CLI lets you ship agents from the terminal

name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
summary: major upgrade to the everyday workhorse model — faster coding, better agent planning, computer use improvements, and 1M token context window in beta

name: Qwen 3
summary: two Apache 2.0 releases: 72B dense and 235B MoE — both free to run locally, rival GPT-class performance for coding and reasoning without cloud costs

name: Llama 4 Scout + Maverick
summary: Meta's first natively multimodal open models — Scout (17B active/109B total MoE, runs on a single H100) and Maverick (17B active/400B total, beats GPT-4o on most benchmarks); video and image understanding, Apache 2.0

name: Cursor 3
summary: rebuilt IDE around parallel AI agents — run many agents at once across local, cloud, worktree, and remote SSH; new Design Mode lets you annotate UI elements directly in the browser

name: Google Gemma 4
summary: four open-weight models (2B–31B) with Apache 2.0 license — runs on a Raspberry Pi or Mac Mini, built from Gemini 3 research, most permissive Gemma release yet
